discordance.evidence: "Lactic acid reported at 46 [sic] from ED attg note" ... "His O2 sats dropped, 90% on NRB with ABG 7.26/41/70" ... "placed on Bipap ___ rising to 98%" ... "FAST ultrasound in the ED showed no gallstones" ... "On Transfer to floor T:95.6 ax 120/65 56 16 98%RA ... ABD: NABS. Soft, NT, ND."

alarm_detect.summary: A patient with severe acute pancreatitis (lipase >3000, acidosis pH 7.26, hypoxia requiring BiPAP, markedly elevated lactate ~46, hemodynamic instability) has transitioned to a soft, non-tender, non-distended abdomen on floor transfer — a strikingly benign exam discordant with the degree of systemic derangement, raising concern for an evolving process (e.g., necrotizing pancreatitis, ischemia, or ongoing occult injury) not yet reflected in the physical exam.

alarm_resolve.reason: A complete abdominal ultrasound can assess biliary anatomy and hepatic parenchyma but cannot resolve the core discordance flag — the mismatch between severe systemic derangement and a benign abdominal exam — which requires cross-sectional imaging (CT or MRI) capable of detecting necrotizing pancreatitis, ischemia, or other occult intra-abdominal catastrophe, and the physician's reasoning is focused entirely on etiology/biliary workup rather than on evaluating for an evolving life-threatening process.
